name: sharing-skills
description: Use when you've developed a broadly useful skill and want to contribute it upstream via pull request - guides process of branching, committing, pushing, and creating PR to contribute skills back to upstream repository
capabilities: Creates feature branches for skill contributions
Commits skill changes with proper format
Creates pull requests to upstream repositories
author: DYAI2025
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing git workflow guidance for skill contributions. The 59 static findings are all false positives: network and filesystem patterns are standard git operations in the documented config directory, external command patterns are inline documentation examples using backticks for shell syntax illustration, and cryptographic algorithm references are analysis artifacts in metadata, not actual cryptographic code.
valueStatement: Contribute your useful skills back to the community. This skill guides you through branching, committing, pushing, and creating a pull request to share skills with upstream repositories.
limitations: Requires gh CLI installed and authenticated
Cannot merge PRs - only creates them
Requires existing fork and upstream configuration

useCases.title: Share useful skills
useCases.description: Contribute tested skills to the upstream repository for community use

useCases.title: Follow git workflow
useCases.description: Properly branch, commit, and PR skills following contribution guidelines

useCases.title: Collaborate on skills
useCases.description: Push changes to fork and create reviewable PRs for skill improvements

promptTemplates.title: Share a new skill
promptTemplates.scenario: I want to contribute a new skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Use sharing-skills to help me contribute my [skill-name] skill to the upstream repository

promptTemplates.title: Update existing skill
promptTemplates.scenario: I need to update a skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Use sharing-skills to help me update [existing-skill-name] and create a PR for the changes

promptTemplates.title: Sync before sharing
promptTemplates.prompt: Use sharing-skills to sync my fork with upstream and prepare a branch for [skill-name]

promptTemplates.title: After PR merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Use sharing-skills to help me clean up my local and remote branches after my PR was merged

bestPractices: Test your skill thoroughly using writing-skills before sharing
Keep PRs focused - one skill per pull request
Include clear testing notes in your commit message
antiPatterns: Batching multiple skills into a single PR
Skipping the sync step before creating a branch

faq.question: What tools do I need installed?
faq.answer: You need gh CLI installed and authenticated with gh auth login. Git is required as well.

faq.question: What directory should I be in?
faq.answer: Your working directory should be ~/.config/superpowers/skills/ where your local skills clone exists.

faq.question: How do I set up my fork?
faq.answer: Configure upstream remote pointing to the main repo and origin pointing to your fork on GitHub.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only executes git commands in your skills directory. No credentials are collected.

faq.question: What if my PR has merge conflicts?
faq.answer: Fetch upstream and rebase on the latest main branch, then resolve conflicts and force push your branch.

faq.question: How is this different from writing-skills?
faq.answer: writing-skills helps you create and test skills. sharing-skills helps you contribute finished skills upstream via PR.
